revision.comment: Disambiguating links to [[Ōta Station]] (link changed to [[Ōta Station (Gunma)]]) using [[User:Qwertyytrewqqwerty/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Tumulus 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka43.html Ōta City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]]
#redirect[[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka43.html Ōta City official site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:imported Kofun]]Nyotaizan Kofun[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:qq 3]][[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nyotaizan Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Tumulus 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Tumulus 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Tumulus 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Kofun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Kofun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Kofun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters) [[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]] ==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Kofun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka43.html Ōta City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
Nyotaizan Kofun

From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ota Gunma Nyotaisan Kofun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|17|31|N|139|23|40|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōta, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka43.html Ōta City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
Nyotaizan Kofun

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have also been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from [[:en:Ōta Station (Gunma)|Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nyotaisan Kofun]][[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]][[ja:女体山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nyotaisan Kofun]][[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]][[ja:女体山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nyotaisan Kofun]][[en:Nyotaizan Kofun]][[ja:女体山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17229664]] Wikidata name: Nyotaisan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nyotaiyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.291861111111|N|139.39433333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have also been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from {{ill|Ōta Station (Gunma)|en|Ōta Station (Gunma)|WD=Q8081729|lt=Ōta Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka43.html Ōta City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nyotaizan Kofun | native_name =女体山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nyotaiyama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nyotaizan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.291861111111|N|139.39433333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179|1=Ōta, Gunma}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nyotaizan kofun'''|女体山古墳|}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162281|title=女体山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest scallop-shaped ''kofun'' (帆立貝形古墳) in Japan after the [[Otomeyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} (128.3 meters) and the [[Osabotsuka Kofun]] in {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} (175 meters)
==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have also been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from {{ill|Ōta Station (Gunma)|en|Ōta Station (Gunma)|WD=Q8081729|lt=Ōta Station|1=Ōta Station (Gunma)}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka43.html Ōta City official site] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Nyotaizan Kofun is located on a small plateau south of the Kanayama hills. It is part of a group of tumuli which includes the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]]. Its main dimensions are:
*Overall length: 106 meters *Posterior circle: 84 meter diameter by 7 meters high *Anterior width: 18 meters by 1 meter high
The tumulus is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and is surrounded by a moat with a width varying from 11 to 19 meters. Numerous cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have also been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was accessed from above, and was pillaged in ancient times; however, the tumulus has not been excavated. From the style, it is estimated that this tumulus dates from the middle of the fifth century AD. It is slightly older than the neighboring Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun, but shares the same orientation, so the two tumuli must be related.
The tumulus is about a 20 minutes walk from {{ill|Ōta Station (Gunma)|en|Ōta Station (Gunma)|WD=Q8081729|lt=Ōta Station|1=Ōta Station (Gunma)}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]].
